i_38_55.speaker: Speaker_M

i_53_44.utterance: I don't know how to start.

i_53_52.speaker: Speaker_M

i_53_53.utterance: For instance, this one time, it had been raining several days and this kid came to me and gave me his last pair of dry socks.  I put them in pocket.  It's only a little thing, but those are the kind of guys I had.

i_53_54.speaker: Speaker_M
i_53_54.utterance: They didn't die, they killed themselves for each other.  I mean that exactly.  I mean, a little more selfish and they'd have been here today.

i_53_55.speaker: Speaker_M
i_53_55.utterance: I got this idea watching 'em go down.  Everything was being destroyed, see, but it seemed that one new thing was made.  A sort of responsibility.  Man for man. You know what I mean?

i_53_56.speaker: Speaker_M

i_53_57.speaker: Speaker_M

i_53_58.speaker: Speaker_M
i_53_58.utterance: And I felt what you said, ashamed somehow 'cause nobody had changed at all.  It seemed to make suckers out of a lot of guys.  I felt wrong to be alive to open the new bank book, drive the new car, see the new refrigerator.

i_53_59.speaker: Speaker_M
i_53_59.utterance: I mean, you can take those things out of a war, but when you drive that car, you've gotta realize that it came out of the love a man can have for a man.  You've gotta be a little better because of it.

i_53_60.utterance: Otherwise all you have is really loot and there's blood on it.  And I didn't want to take any of that.  And I guess that included you.

i_57_1.utterance: You knew there was nothing in that.

i_57_2.speaker: Speaker_M
i_57_2.utterance: I knew nothing of the sort. You took presents from him.

i_57_3.speaker: Speaker_F
i_57_3.utterance: Presents, just a trivial little broach.

i_57_4.speaker: Speaker_M
i_57_4.utterance: Yes, I remember it well, bristling with diamonds in the worst possible taste.

i_57_5.speaker: Speaker_F
i_57_5.utterance: No it wasn't. It was very pretty I still have it and I wear it often.

i_57_6.speaker: Speaker_M
i_57_6.utterance: You went out of your way to torture me over Peter Burden.

i_57_7.speaker: Speaker_F
i_57_7.utterance: No I didn't, you worked the whole thing up in your jealous imagination.

i_57_8.speaker: Speaker_M
i_57_8.utterance: You must admit he was in love with you, wasn't he?

i_57_9.speaker: Speaker_F
i_57_9.utterance: Just a little perhaps, nothing serious.

i_57_10.speaker: Speaker_M
i_57_10.utterance: You let him kiss you; you said you did.

i_57_13.speaker: Speaker_F
i_57_13.utterance: It gave him a lot of pleasure, it didn't hurt me any.

i_57_15.speaker: Speaker_F
i_57_15.utterance: Well, if you hadn't been so nosey and suspicious you never wouldn't have known anything about it.

i_57_16.speaker: Speaker_M
i_57_16.utterance: That's a nice point of view I must say.

i_57_18.speaker: Speaker_M
i_57_18.utterance: Yes, me too, bored stiff. You want some Brandy?

i_57_20.speaker: Speaker_M
i_57_20.utterance: I have a little I think.

i_57_21.speaker: Speaker_F
i_57_21.utterance: I don't see why you want any you've already had two glasses.

i_57_22.speaker: Speaker_M
i_57_22.utterance: No particular reason. Anyhow, they are very small ones.

i_57_23.speaker: Speaker_F
i_57_23.utterance: It just seems so silly to go on and on and on with such a thing.

i_57_24.speaker: Speaker_M
i_57_24.utterance: You can hardly call three liquor glasses in a whole evening going on and on and on.

i_57_25.speaker: Speaker_F
i_57_25.utterance: Well, it's become a habit with you.

i_57_26.speaker: Speaker_M
i_57_26.utterance: You needn't be so grand just because don't happen want any yourself right now.

i_57_32.speaker: Speaker_F
i_57_32.utterance: No I'm just making myself fascinating for you.

i_57_33.speaker: Speaker_M
i_57_33.utterance: That reply has broken my heart.

i_57_34.speaker: Speaker_F
i_57_34.utterance: It's a woman's job to allure the man

i_57_35.speaker: Speaker_F
i_57_35.utterance: watch me a minute, will you?

i_57_36.speaker: Speaker_M
i_57_36.utterance: As a matter of fact, that's perfectly true.

i_57_40.speaker: Speaker_M
i_57_40.utterance: It's a pity you didn't have any more Brandy it might have made you a little less disagreeable.

i_57_41.speaker: Speaker_F
i_57_41.utterance: It doesn't seem to work such wonders with you.

i_57_42.speaker: Speaker_M
i_57_42.utterance: Snap, snap, snap, like a little adder.

i_57_44.speaker: Speaker_M
i_57_44.utterance: Oh nonsense they have a little bag of venom behind their fangs and they snap.

i_57_47.speaker: Speaker_F
i_57_47.utterance: I don't care. Do you understand? I don't care. I don't care if they bark or roll about like hoops.

i_57_48.speaker: Speaker_M
i_57_48.utterance: Tell me, did you see much of Peter Burden after our divorce?

i_57_49.speaker: Speaker_F
i_57_49.utterance: Yes, I did quite a lot.

i_57_50.speaker: Speaker_M
i_57_50.utterance: I imagine you let him kiss you good deal more then.

i_57_52.speaker: Speaker_M
i_57_52.utterance: What riotous time you must have had.

i_57_53.speaker: Speaker_M
i_57_53.utterance: No restraint at all, very enjoyable, never had much anyhow.

i_57_54.speaker: Speaker_F
i_57_54.utterance: You're quite insufferable. I expect it's because you're drunk.

i_57_55.speaker: Speaker_M
i_57_55.utterance: I am not in the least drunk.

i_57_56.speaker: Speaker_M
i_57_56.utterance: I imagine, as I may have already mentioned, I've had three minute liquor glasses of Brandy the whole evening long a child of two couldn't get drunk on that.

i_57_57.speaker: Speaker_F
i_57_57.utterance: On the contrary a child of two could get violently drunk on only one glass of Brandy.

i_57_58.speaker: Speaker_M
i_57_58.utterance: Oh very interesting. How about a child of four, a child of six or a child of nine?

i_57_60.speaker: Speaker_M
i_57_60.utterance: We can get a good little debate going about that, don't you think? Intemperate tots.

i_57_61.speaker: Speaker_F
i_57_61.utterance: Not very funny my dear, you better have some more Brandy.

i_57_63.speaker: Speaker_M
i_57_63.utterance: Better turn that off I think.

i_57_65.utterance: Because it's late and it'll annoy the people upstairs.

i_57_66.speaker: Speaker_F
i_57_66.utterance: There aren't any people upstairs it's a photographer's studio.

i_57_67.speaker: Speaker_M
i_57_67.utterance: There are people downstairs I suppose?

i_57_69.speaker: Speaker_M
i_57_69.utterance: This is no time of the year for Tunis.

i_57_72.speaker: Speaker_F
i_57_72.utterance: Very well, if you insist on being boorish and idiotic.

i_57_73.speaker: Speaker_M
i_57_73.utterance: Turn it off it's driving me mad.

i_57_74.speaker: Speaker_F
i_57_74.utterance: You are far too temperamental. Try to control yourself.

i_57_76.speaker: Speaker_F
i_57_76.utterance: Stop it. Stop it. I hate you.

i_57_78.speaker: Speaker_F
i_57_78.utterance: Listen indeed. I'm sick and tired of listening to you, damn sadistic bully.

i_57_80.speaker: Speaker_F
i_57_80.utterance: Stop it. Stop it. I hate you, do you hear? You're overbearing and conceited and utterly impossible.

i_57_81.speaker: Speaker_M
i_57_81.utterance: You're a vile tempered, loose living, wicked little beast and I hope I never see you again as long as I live.

i_57_82.speaker: Speaker_F
i_57_82.utterance: This is the end, do you hear? The end finally and forever.

i_57_86.speaker: Speaker_F
i_57_86.utterance: Yes, I am. Let go of me you're cruel fiend and I hate and loathe you. Thank God I realized who you really are before I would ever, ever marry you again. I would never marry you again. I'd rather die in torment.

i_57_89.speaker: Speaker_M
i_57_89.utterance: I wouldn't marry you again if you came to me on your bended knees. You're a evil minded little vampire. I hope to God I never see you again as long as I live.

i_57_90.speaker: Speaker_F
i_57_90.utterance: You're a beast. I hate you. Stop it. Stop it.

i_58_0.speaker: Speaker_F
i_58_0.utterance: God, finally I got to the line, the front of the line. I've been waiting there for like half an hour.

i_58_1.speaker: Speaker_F
i_58_1.utterance: [BREATHING] It's so hot in here. Hoo.

i_58_5.speaker: Speaker_M
i_58_5.utterance: You didn't fill this out right. All right. It says that you have to fill out the back sheet of this, but you filled out the yellow sheet of it.

i_58_6.speaker: Speaker_F
i_58_6.utterance: No, no, no, no, no, no. The lady over there, she told me to fill out the front and she said to wait in this line.

i_58_8.speaker: Speaker_F
i_58_8.utterance: I don't know. Some lady. Sonya or something.

i_58_9.speaker: Speaker_M
i_58_9.utterance: She- she probably doesn't even work here.

i_58_11.speaker: Speaker_F
i_58_11.utterance: Excuse me? No, no. I waited in the line over there..

i_58_12.speaker: Speaker_F
i_58_12.utterance: and she told me to come over here and she said to fill out everything on the application and I did that.

i_58_14.speaker: Speaker_F
i_58_14.utterance: Look, I-- No, no, no, I--you don't understand. I have to go back to work.

i_58_16.speaker: Speaker_M
i_58_16.utterance: Well, you didn't fill this paper at work out right and it's going to take another fifteen minutes to do it and look at how long the line we got here so just-

i_58_18.speaker: Speaker_M
i_58_18.utterance: okay, just go back and fill it out real quick and I'm sorry, you have to get back in line and wait. I can't let you cut.

i_58_19.speaker: Speaker_F
i_58_19.utterance: But you don't understand. I cannot get back in line because I have to go back to work. I will get fired if I don't leave here.

i_58_20.speaker: Speaker_M
i_58_20.utterance: Well, maybe you should have got here earlier than so you had a lot of time to do this.

i_58_21.speaker: Speaker_F
i_58_21.utterance: But I was at work and the lady told me to come and work it and to stand in the line.

i_58_22.speaker: Speaker_M
i_58_22.utterance: I don't know who this lady is.

i_59_4.speaker: Speaker_F
i_59_4.utterance: Hey listen,eh- I've been wanting to talk to you. I have something to tell you. How's Amy doing?

i_59_5.speaker: Speaker_M
i_59_5.utterance: She's doing fine. She just took her first steps today actually. I videotaped it for you so you can watch it.

i_59_8.speaker: Speaker_F
i_59_8.utterance: I really want to say that. Well I, so, you know, at the center department they called me in and I talked to-

i_59_9.speaker: Speaker_M
i_59_9.utterance: Oh, yeah? What did they say?

i_59_10.speaker: Speaker_F
i_59_10.utterance: Well umm- actually they--I was promoted.

i_59_12.speaker: Speaker_F
i_59_12.utterance: And Uh- Yeah. Uh- They're going to need um- more nurses on call for uh- to go overseas to help out with the war.

i_59_13.speaker: Speaker_F
i_59_13.utterance: And uh - seeing as I was uh- promoted and head of the department now, and so- well, they asked me to go.

i_59_16.speaker: Speaker_F
i_59_16.utterance: It's going- It's going to be probably in about three weeks.

i_59_17.speaker: Speaker_M
i_59_17.utterance: What? You can't do that. Three weeks?

i_59_19.speaker: Speaker_F
i_59_19.utterance: Well, I'm leaving in three weeks, but-- Yeah.

i_59_20.speaker: Speaker_M
i_59_20.utterance: God, we're supposed to take a look at a house next week.

i_59_21.speaker: Speaker_F
i_59_21.utterance: Maybe we can still do that

i_59_22.speaker: Speaker_M
i_59_22.utterance: I can't- No, I can't do this alone.

i_59_24.speaker: Speaker_M
i_59_24.utterance: Amy's starting school in a month.

i_59_25.speaker: Speaker_F
i_59_25.utterance: I know, but look, I have to take this. I mean, if I don't, I mean, I could get fired.

i_59_26.speaker: Speaker_F
i_59_26.utterance: And we know you need-we need the money. We couldn't even- purchase a house if I didn't take this.

i_59_28.utterance: You know what happened to my cousin. She went and she never came back.

i_59_29.speaker: Speaker_F
i_59_29.utterance: Honey, I'm going to come back. It's not like that.

i_59_30.speaker: Speaker_F
i_59_30.utterance: But it- it is for a year.

i_59_32.speaker: Speaker_F
i_59_32.utterance: Babe, I'm sorry. But I don't want to do.

i_59_33.speaker: Speaker_F
i_59_33.utterance: You know as much as -as much as me that I-I want to stay here with you and Amy so bad, but I can't.

i_59_35.speaker: Speaker_M
i_59_35.utterance: You remember the last time you went? You know how hard it was for me? Now we have a kid.

i_59_36.speaker: Speaker_F
i_59_36.utterance: But you're so good with her. You know, she knows you better than she knows me 'cause I'm at work every day.

i_59_40.speaker: Speaker_M
i_59_40.utterance: You can't leave your daughter alone like this.

i_59_41.speaker: Speaker_F
i_59_41.utterance: But she has you. I mean, that's what you're there for. You're my right-hand man.

i_59_45.utterance: Wait, you can write me and as you know, send me pictures.

i_59_46.speaker: Speaker_M
i_59_46.utterance: I don't know if I can handle you going another year.

i_59_48.speaker: Speaker_M
i_59_48.utterance: I don't know if I- if I could take it.

i_59_49.speaker: Speaker_F
i_59_49.utterance: Look, I need you to be strong for me. 'Cause I can't be the only one that's strong right now, really, I--

i_59_50.speaker: Speaker_M
i_59_50.utterance: I can't be that strong. A whole year.

i_59_52.speaker: Speaker_F
i_59_52.utterance: But we can work, you know, we--you can write me and we could see pictures

i_59_53.speaker: Speaker_F
i_59_53.utterance: and if we survive, if we survive that then babe, you know you know we could survive any--

i_59_54.speaker: Speaker_M
i_59_54.utterance: What if you don't come back?

i_59_56.speaker: Speaker_M
i_59_56.utterance: What am I supposed--oh, one out of five people aren't coming back.

i_59_58.speaker: Speaker_F
i_59_58.utterance: I can do it. I'm going to do it. And I'll be back safe and sound and-

i_59_59.speaker: Speaker_F
i_59_59.utterance: we'll have all the money that we ever needed.

i_59_60.speaker: Speaker_F
i_59_60.utterance: And- And then I'll stop working and- I'll stay home with the baby.

i_59_61.speaker: Speaker_M
i_59_61.utterance: I don't want the money. I want-you here

i_59_62.speaker: Speaker_M
i_59_62.utterance: we'll get money, all right? You don't need the job, all right?

i_59_63.speaker: Speaker_M
i_59_63.utterance: If your life is going to be in danger and this family is going to be in danger we don't need the job. We'll find the money.

i_59_64.speaker: Speaker_F
i_59_64.utterance: It's all I ever knew how to do. I can't quit now.

i_59_65.speaker: Speaker_M
i_59_65.utterance: How are we supposed to tell Amy? How's she supposed to understand that?

i_59_66.speaker: Speaker_F
i_59_66.utterance: Well, we- we tell her mommy's going on va- on a trip for for- a while.

i_59_67.utterance: How is she going to feel the first year in school, no mom.

i_59_69.speaker: Speaker_F
i_59_69.utterance: It's hard enough already, Brian, I can't.

i_59_70.speaker: Speaker_M
i_59_70.utterance: I'm not thinking about just me; I'm thinking about our child.

i_59_72.speaker: Speaker_F
i_59_72.utterance: I didn't expect this to happen. I never planned for this. Some things you just don't plan for, you know.

i_60_0.speaker: Speaker_M
i_60_0.utterance: Oh, my God, look at that. Huh-oh, how many carats is that?

i_60_1.speaker: Speaker_F
i_60_1.utterance: I know. [LAUGHTER]. Dude, I don't know. It's- Probably. It's huge. I don't even know how to like wear it. My hand is like going down like really low. I can't believe it.

i_60_6.speaker: Speaker_M
i_60_6.utterance: So how did he do it?

i_60_7.speaker: Speaker_F
i_60_7.utterance: Oh, man. So I was--okay, so we went to go see this show on Broadway, right? And it was The wed- the wed- The Lousy Chaperone. Have you seen that one?

i_60_9.speaker: Speaker_M
i_60_9.utterance: Yeah, The Lousy Chaperone. [LAUGHTER] Uh huh.

i_60_10.speaker: Speaker_F
i_60_10.utterance: Right. Right. Right. So, and there's like a whole like wedding scene in it. All right, not the--Drowsy, Drowsy [LAUGHTER] --not the Lousy. [LAUGHTER]. Um - So Drowsy. So I go to see this Drowsy Chaperone. And uh-

i_60_12.speaker: Speaker_F
i_60_12.utterance: at the end of the performance, you know, like we're all clapping and stuff. And all of a sudden I look over and

i_60_13.speaker: Speaker_F
i_60_13.utterance: Joe's gone. -where did he go, you know?

i_60_15.speaker: Speaker_F
i_60_15.utterance: And I'm like oh, he went to the- -the bathroom, but he didn't tell me, I didn't even notice him leaving. And all of a sudden, I swear to God, I'm sitting there and-

i_60_16.speaker: Speaker_F
i_60_16.utterance: he comes up onstage-- And the entire cast everyone just like

i_60_18.speaker: Speaker_F
i_60_18.utterance: goes up there and everyone's clapping and he's standing there and they put a spotlight on him.

i_60_20.speaker: Speaker_F
i_60_20.utterance: And I'm like freaking out. I'm like what the hell's going on, you know. I don't know what's going on. And so he's like standing there and then all of a sudden they give him a microphone-

i_60_21.speaker: Speaker_F
i_60_21.utterance: and he just starts doing like this little spiel about me and like

i_60_22.speaker: Speaker_F
i_60_22.utterance: he asked me to marry him, like- onstage. I was like, I couldn't believe--and I had to walk up there and I was, like I think I tripped or something.

i_60_24.speaker: Speaker_M
i_60_24.utterance: Wha- What'd you say? You said yes, right? [LAUGHTER]

i_60_25.speaker: Speaker_F
i_60_25.utterance: Yeah, I said yes, but I, yeah, I mean,

i_60_26.speaker: Speaker_F
i_60_26.utterance: I don't know- it just like took me, took me by surprise.

i_60_28.speaker: Speaker_M
i_60_28.utterance: Did you get a standing ovation?

i_60_29.speaker: Speaker_F
i_60_29.utterance: Oh, yeah. Everyone--yeah. And people--friends of ours were in the audience, I didn't know about, like family members.

i_60_34.speaker: Speaker_M
i_60_34.utterance: No, you deserve to cry. I mean, tears of happiness.

i_60_35.speaker: Speaker_F
i_60_35.utterance: But, I don't know, I--it's so weird to think I'm marrying somebody 'cause I feel so young, you know?

i_60_36.speaker: Speaker_M
i_60_36.utterance: So where- where- where is this--have you talked about where it's going to be?

i_60_37.speaker: Speaker_F
i_60_37.utterance: No. No plans. or any- and probably

i_60_38.speaker: Speaker_F
i_60_38.utterance: like I think I want to get married--I want to have two weddings. One here, then one in Europe.

i_60_40.utterance: No. Hawaii's for honeymoons. But uh--

i_60_41.speaker: Speaker_M
i_60_41.utterance: Oh. Like Paris? On the Eiffel Tower?

i_60_42.speaker: Speaker_F
i_60_42.utterance: Oh, maybe. I don't know. I've been there so many times. I think it's--we're going to get married in Spain and then we're going to get married here.

i_60_43.speaker: Speaker_M
i_60_43.utterance: He's rich enough. Maybe you can rent out the Eiffel Tower for your--

i_61_0.speaker: Speaker_F
i_61_0.utterance: I don't know, Brian, if they uh--it's been three years.

i_61_1.speaker: Speaker_M
i_61_1.utterance: Hey, you just got to keep on going.

i_61_2.speaker: Speaker_F
i_61_2.utterance: No, I don't know, I--maybe this isn't for me. You know, maybe this isn't the right business for me.

i_61_4.speaker: Speaker_F
i_61_4.utterance: I just, I see all these other people getting commercials and work and it's like I'm not getting anything.

i_61_5.speaker: Speaker_F
i_61_5.utterance: I have no money behind me. Nothing. I'm twenty five and--

i_61_6.speaker: Speaker_M
i_61_6.utterance: Russel Crowe wasn't forty until he got his big stuff.

i_61_7.speaker: Speaker_F
i_61_7.utterance: So I'll have to wait twenty five years-

i_61_8.speaker: Speaker_F
i_61_8.utterance: -just going and auditioning and auditioning and rejection after refection, it's like--

i_61_9.speaker: Speaker_M
i_61_9.utterance: If you really want to do it-I mean,

i_61_11.speaker: Speaker_F
i_61_11.utterance: I know, I just don't know if I can handle it. I mean, you're helping me so much financially it's like I can't survive on my own without you. I don't like that.

i_61_12.speaker: Speaker_M
i_61_12.utterance: You could. You could. We just--

i_61_13.speaker: Speaker_M
i_61_13.utterance: we're just pooling our funds together.

i_61_15.speaker: Speaker_F
i_61_15.utterance: I feel like I'm not doing anything. Like I'm not giving anything into this world.

i_61_16.speaker: Speaker_M
i_61_16.utterance: You are, you're going to class, you're learning, you're getting better. When you finally get that role you're going to be prepared.

i_61_17.speaker: Speaker_F
i_61_17.utterance: Yeah, but what if I never make it? What if I studied all my life, this theatre, this acting, and I'm never going to get anywhere. I'm never going to be able to have a job.

i_61_18.utterance: Oh my God, Anna, you're still young. Don't even think about that.

i_61_19.utterance: Time is passing by. Every year it's

i_61_20.utterance: a part left than I can do.

i_61_21.utterance: You're only twenty five, okay? Some people don't start until they're thirty five.

i_61_24.utterance: I don't know, I just [BREATHING] I get frustrated

i_61_25.utterance: What would you do- What would you do if you didn't? You can go to law school.

i_61_26.speaker: Speaker_F
i_61_26.utterance: That's the thing. I don't know. I don't know anything.

i_61_28.speaker: Speaker_F
i_61_28.utterance: I don't know. I don't know anything other than this, though, is the problem. This is my life. This is what I built everything about, you know.

i_61_29.speaker: Speaker_M
i_61_29.utterance: See? So you can't just quit now.

i_61_30.speaker: Speaker_F
i_61_30.utterance: I mean, I could go to law school, but I'm just not passionate about it, you know? Like I'm not--it doesn't drive me, it doesn't motivate me. I--

i_61_33.speaker: Speaker_F
i_61_33.utterance: I just, I need a break. I need something to break me out.

i_61_34.utterance: How's the job? Still waiting tables at Denny's?

i_61_36.speaker: Speaker_F

i_61_39.speaker: Speaker_F

i_61_42.speaker: Speaker_M

i_61_44.utterance: Close to thirty. Oof, it's going to be tough.

i_62_1.speaker: Speaker_M
i_62_1.utterance: I'm sorry, Mrs. Rosenkrantz, but uh- I don't know what to tell you.

i_66_57.utterance: You're a considerate fella and there's nothing wrong in that.

i_66_62.speaker: Speaker_M
i_66_62.utterance: I wanted to get this settled first.

i_66_63.speaker: Speaker_F
i_66_63.utterance: Well, how do you know she's going to marry you? I mean, what if she feels the same way as your father?

i_66_64.speaker: Speaker_M
i_66_64.utterance: Well then I guess it's over then. But from her letters I think she's forgotten him.

i_66_65.speaker: Speaker_M
i_66_65.utterance: I'll find out. But then after it's all over we'll thrash it out with dad, right?

i_66_67.speaker: Speaker_F
i_66_67.utterance: You know, the trouble is you've never had enough women. No, you never did.

i_66_68.speaker: Speaker_M
i_66_68.utterance: So what? I'm not fast with women.

i_66_69.speaker: Speaker_F
i_66_69.utterance: I don't understand why it has to be her.

i_66_71.speaker: Speaker_F
i_66_71.utterance: That's a good answer, but it doesn't answer anything.

i_66_73.speaker: Speaker_F
i_66_73.utterance: I mean, you haven't seen her since the war. I mean, it's been five years.

i_66_75.speaker: Speaker_M
i_66_75.utterance: I grew up with her. Okay. All these years, when I think of someone I want to be my wife I think of Annie. What do you want, a diagram?

i_66_76.speaker: Speaker_F
i_66_76.utterance: No, I don't want a diagram.

i_66_77.speaker: Speaker_F
i_66_77.utterance: He thinks he's coming back. You go-go on and marry her, I mean, you're pronouncing her dead. And what's going to happen to your father, huh? I don't know. Do you?

i_66_79.speaker: Speaker_F
i_66_79.utterance: Just, listen. Give it more thought, is all.

i_66_80.speaker: Speaker_M
i_66_80.utterance: No, I've given it three years of thought.

i_66_81.speaker: Speaker_M
i_66_81.utterance: And I thought that if I waited then dad would forget about Larry and then we could have a regular wedding and everything happy.

i_66_82.speaker: Speaker_M
i_66_82.utterance: But if I can't have that here then I'll get out.

i_66_84.speaker: Speaker_M
i_66_84.utterance: I'll get out. I'll marry her somewhere else. Maybe New York.

i_66_86.speaker: Speaker_M
i_66_86.utterance: I've been a sucker too long. I'm through with it.

i_66_87.speaker: Speaker_F
i_66_87.utterance: You're what-you're going to leave the business? But you can't do that. What the hell is this?

i_66_88.speaker: Speaker_M
i_66_88.utterance: The business. The business doesn't inspire me.

i_66_89.speaker: Speaker_F
i_66_89.utterance: Oh, you have to be inspired.

i_66_90.speaker: Speaker_M
i_66_90.utterance: Yes. I like it an hour out of the day. If I have to grub for money all day long at least that evening I want it beautiful. I want a wife. I want a family. I want some kids. I want to build something I can give myself to.

i_66_91.speaker: Speaker_M
i_66_91.utterance: Now, Annie's in the middle of all that.

i_66_92.speaker: Speaker_M
i_66_92.utterance: Now, where do I find it?

i_66_98.speaker: Speaker_F
i_66_98.utterance: All right, but you can't do that. You can't talk like that.

i_66_100.speaker: Speaker_F
i_66_100.utterance: We've worked all our lives for you. Only for you, Chris. The whole shootin' match is for you.

i_66_101.speaker: Speaker_M
i_66_101.utterance: Yeah, I know, I know, just help me stay here, all right?

i_66_103.speaker: Speaker_F
i_66_103.utterance: You just can't talk like that.

i_66_105.speaker: Speaker_F
i_66_105.utterance: I don't know you, do I?

i_66_106.speaker: Speaker_M
i_66_106.utterance: No, you don't. I'm a pretty tough guy.

i_67_0.speaker: Speaker_F
i_67_0.utterance: Why did you invite her here?

i_67_2.speaker: Speaker_F
i_67_2.utterance: She's been in New York for three-and-a-half years. She don't just suddenly come and-

i_67_3.speaker: Speaker_M
i_67_3.utterance: Well, maybe, maybe he just wanted to see her.

i_67_4.speaker: Speaker_F
i_67_4.utterance: You don't travel seven hundred miles just to see.

i_67_5.speaker: Speaker_M
i_67_5.utterance: What do you mean? They lived next door to each other their entire lives. Why shouldn't he want to see her? Hey, don't look at me like that. I don't know anymore than you do.

i_67_6.speaker: Speaker_F
i_67_6.utterance: He's not going to marry her.

i_67_7.speaker: Speaker_M
i_67_7.utterance: How do you know he's even thinking about it?

i_67_12.speaker: Speaker_F
i_67_12.utterance: She's not Larry's girl. She knows that.

i_67_14.speaker: Speaker_F
i_67_14.utterance: Then why is she married? Then why isn't she married? She's been in New York and there's hundreds of single men there.

i_67_15.speaker: Speaker_F
i_67_15.utterance: I'm sure hundreds of them asked her and told her that she's foolish, but no.

i_67_17.speaker: Speaker_M
i_67_17.utterance: How do you know why she's waited?

i_67_18.speaker: Speaker_F
i_67_18.utterance: Because she thinks like me, that's why.

i_67_19.speaker: Speaker_F
i_67_19.utterance: She's faithful as a rock, don't you know. In my worst moments I can think of her-

i_67_20.speaker: Speaker_F
i_67_20.utterance: I think again, that I'm-I'm right.

i_67_21.speaker: Speaker_M
i_67_21.utterance: Look, it's a nice day. What are we arguing for?

i_67_22.speaker: Speaker_F
i_67_22.utterance: Nobody in my house better ever dare-dare to challenge her faith. Maybe strangers, but not his father, not his brother.

i_67_24.speaker: Speaker_F
i_67_24.utterance: Because he's not coming back. Then what do I got to live for? I'll kill myself.

i_67_26.speaker: Speaker_F
i_67_26.utterance: Don't, don't laugh at me, don't laugh at me.

i_67_27.speaker: Speaker_F
i_67_27.utterance: Look at it. Because why, when she came here and she slept in his room, the memorial broke into pieces. Look at it, look at it, Joe.

i_67_28.speaker: Speaker_M
i_67_28.utterance: All right. All right. Calm yourself.

i_67_29.speaker: Speaker_F
i_67_29.utterance: You got to believe, believe with me because I can't stand all alone.

i_67_32.speaker: Speaker_F
i_67_32.utterance: Believe, Joe. You above all people have got to believe.

i_67_39.speaker: Speaker_M
i_67_39.utterance: What have I got to hide?

i_67_40.speaker: Speaker_M
i_67_40.utterance: Huh? What the hell's the matter with you, Kate?

i_68_0.speaker: Speaker_F
i_68_0.utterance: You're the only one I know that still loves his parents.

i_68_1.speaker: Speaker_M
i_68_1.utterance: Yeah. I know. It went out of style, didn't it?

i_68_2.speaker: Speaker_F
i_68_2.utterance: No, it's all right. There's nothing wrong in that, you know.

i_68_3.speaker: Speaker_F
i_68_3.utterance: It's really lovely here. The air is sweet.

i_68_8.speaker: Speaker_F
i_68_8.utterance: Well, for one thing, your mother so much has told me to go. [LAUGHTER].

i_68_11.speaker: Speaker_F
i_68_11.utterance: you've been acting kind of, well, you know.

i_68_13.speaker: Speaker_F
i_68_13.utterance: Well, you've been embarrassed ever since I came.

i_68_14.speaker: Speaker_M
i_68_14.utterance: Yes, I guess I have been. But it's going from me now.

i_68_15.speaker: Speaker_M
i_68_15.utterance: Oh look, um, well, the thing is, I was planning on, planning on sort of sneaking up on you over a week or so, and they-they take it for granted we're all set.

i_68_17.speaker: Speaker_F
i_68_17.utterance: Yeah, they would. Especially your mom.

i_68_20.speaker: Speaker_F
i_68_20.utterance: from her opinion, why else would I come here?

i_68_21.speaker: Speaker_M
i_68_21.utterance: Well, uh- would you want to?

i_68_22.speaker: Speaker_M
i_68_22.utterance: I, I guess that's why I asked you to come.

i_68_23.speaker: Speaker_F
i_68_23.utterance: Well, I guess that's why I came.

i_68_26.speaker: Speaker_M
i_68_26.utterance: I love you a great deal.

i_68_27.speaker: Speaker_M
i_68_27.utterance: I-I love you. I have no imagination. That's all I know how to tell it.

i_68_28.speaker: Speaker_M
i_68_28.utterance: I'm embarrassing you. I didn't want to tell you it here. I wanted to go somewhere that would be brand-new to each other. It feels wrong here, don't you? This yard and this chair.

i_68_29.speaker: Speaker_M
i_68_29.utterance: I don't want to win you from anybody. I want you to be ready for me.

i_68_30.speaker: Speaker_F
i_68_30.utterance: I've been ready for a long, long time.

i_68_31.speaker: Speaker_M
i_68_31.utterance: Then he's gone forever. You're sure?

i_68_33.speaker: Speaker_F
i_68_33.utterance: I was almost married two years ago.

i_68_36.speaker: Speaker_M
i_68_36.utterance: You felt something that far back?

i_68_38.speaker: Speaker_M
i_68_38.utterance: Why didn't you let me know?

i_68_39.speaker: Speaker_F
i_68_39.utterance: Well, I don't know, you didn't write until then and then when you did, what'd you say?

i_68_40.speaker: Speaker_F
i_68_40.utterance: You always can be so ambiguous.

i_68_43.speaker: Speaker_M
i_68_43.utterance: [LAUGHTER]. God, I kissed Annie. I kissed Annie. How long, how long I've been waiting to kiss you.

i_68_44.speaker: Speaker_F
i_68_44.utterance: I don't know if I ever can forgive you for that. Why'd you wait so long? I sat in my room wondering if I was crazy for thinking about you.

i_68_45.speaker: Speaker_M
i_68_45.utterance: We're going to live now. I'm going to make you so happy.

i_68_48.speaker: Speaker_F
i_68_48.utterance: Like Larry's brother. Do it like you, Chris.

i_68_50.speaker: Speaker_M
i_68_50.utterance: Let's drive somewhere. I want to be alone with you.

i_68_51.speaker: Speaker_F
i_68_51.utterance: No. What is it? Is it your mother?

i_68_52.speaker: Speaker_M
i_68_52.utterance: No, no, no, no. Nothing like that.

i_68_53.speaker: Speaker_F
i_68_53.utterance: Well then tell me what's wrong. I mean, even in your letters there's something ashamed.

i_68_54.speaker: Speaker_M
i_68_54.utterance: Yeah. I guess there is. I guess I was, but uh- it's going from me now.

i_68_56.speaker: Speaker_M
i_68_56.utterance: I don't know where to start.

i_68_57.speaker: Speaker_F
i_68_57.utterance: But, Chris, it wouldn't work like this.

i_68_58.speaker: Speaker_M
i_68_58.utterance: You know when I went overseas. I said I was in command of a company.

i_68_64.speaker: Speaker_M
i_68_64.utterance: It takes a little while to toss that off. Because they weren't just men.

i_68_65.speaker: Speaker_M
i_68_65.utterance: For instance, one time, it had to have been raining several days. There was this one kid, he came up to me and he gave me his last pair of dry socks. Put them in my pocket.

i_68_66.speaker: Speaker_M
i_68_66.utterance: I mean, that's-that's only a little thing, but that's the kind of guys I had. They didn't just die. They killed themselves for each other. And I mean that exactly. A little more selfish and they'd still be here today.

i_68_67.speaker: Speaker_M
i_68_67.utterance: And I, and I thought something watching them go down.

i_68_68.speaker: Speaker_M
i_68_68.utterance: Everything was being destroyed, see, but there was one new thing that was being created. A kind of responsibility, man for man. You understand me?

i_68_69.speaker: Speaker_M
i_68_69.utterance: And to show that-to bring that onto the earth like some sort of monument standing right behind us where everyone could feel it and it would make a difference to him.

i_68_70.speaker: Speaker_M
i_68_70.utterance: And I came back here and it was incredible. There was no meaning in that here. The whole thing to them was a bus accident.

i_68_71.speaker: Speaker_M
i_68_71.utterance: And I went to work with dad and that rat race again. And I felt what you said-ashamed somehow. Because nobody was changed at all. It seemed to make a sucker out of a lot of guys.

i_68_72.speaker: Speaker_M
i_68_72.utterance: And I felt wrong to be alive. To drive the car, to see the new checkbook, to see the new refrigerator. I mean, you can take those things out of the war. But you got to know that when you drive that car that that came out of the love a man can have for another man. And you got to be a little bit better because of that.

i_68_73.speaker: Speaker_M
i_68_73.utterance: Otherwise, what you have here is loot; and there's blood on it.

i_68_74.speaker: Speaker_M
i_68_74.utterance: And I didn't want to take any of it.

i_68_75.speaker: Speaker_M
i_68_75.utterance: And I guess that included you.

i_68_76.speaker: Speaker_F
i_68_76.utterance: And you still feel that way?

i_68_78.speaker: Speaker_F
i_68_78.utterance: Because you can't feel that way, never again, Chris. You hear me? Never. I mean, the money and me too.

i_68_79.speaker: Speaker_F
i_68_79.utterance: I mean, there's nothing wrong in that money, and your father put good planes up in the air and you should be proud of that. A man should be paid for that.

i_68_81.speaker: Speaker_M
i_68_81.utterance: Annie, I'm going to make a fortune for you.

i_68_82.speaker: Speaker_F
i_68_82.utterance: What am I going to do with a fortune?

i_69_0.speaker: Speaker_M
i_69_0.utterance: Hey, what time is it? They're supposed to run around midnight, right? God, this is great, isn't it?

i_69_1.speaker: Speaker_M
i_69_1.utterance: Look at the night we got; couldn't be better.

i_69_2.speaker: Speaker_M
i_69_2.utterance: You know actually, I wanted to go a little further up the coast to get away from all the, the people and the lights, and uh- but I thought we'd miss it. Hey, how you doing?

i_69_4.speaker: Speaker_M
i_69_4.utterance: Oh, is that-is that-now, is that just foam, uhh, I can't even tell.

i_69_5.speaker: Speaker_M
i_69_5.utterance: Oh, though if I can't tell it probably isn't them, right? It probably would be unmistakable, wouldn't you think? Hey, how you doing?

i_69_6.speaker: Speaker_M
i_69_6.utterance: Oh, do you know what I forgot? I forgot my flashlight. How could I be so stupid. Oh, I-I should have brought my flashlight.

i_69_8.speaker: Speaker_M
i_69_8.utterance: Uh- you know, the flashlight. The silver one. There's only one, isn't there?

i_69_9.speaker: Speaker_F
i_69_9.utterance: That's not your flashlight; it's ours. It's mine too.

i_69_11.speaker: Speaker_F
i_69_11.utterance: I don't understand why you're always saying oh, it's mine, it's mine. It's not like it's just your flashlight. I mean, how do you think that makes me feel?

i_69_12.speaker: Speaker_M
i_69_12.utterance: No, of course. Yours and mine. Our flashlight. Yours and mine. Mi flashlight is su flashlight. Naturally.

i_69_13.speaker: Speaker_M
i_69_13.utterance: We should have brought our flashlight. I can't believe we forgot to bring it.

i_69_14.speaker: Speaker_M
i_69_14.utterance: I hope the moon stays out.

i_69_15.speaker: Speaker_M
i_69_15.utterance: Hey, do they always run during a full moon? I can't remember if it was full last year. Do you remember?

i_69_17.speaker: Speaker_M
i_69_17.utterance: Are you cold? Do you want my jacket?

i_69_18.speaker: Speaker_M
i_69_18.utterance: We should have brought the blanket. Oh, why didn't we bring the blanket? Our blanket.

i_69_19.speaker: Speaker_M
i_69_19.utterance: You know what would be good right now? A six-pack. Ah, I can't believe I forgot a six-pack. A six-pack would be just the ticket right about now.

i_69_20.speaker: Speaker_M
i_69_20.utterance: I'm so stupid. I'm like a little kid. I-it's amazing I get out of the house with my fly zipped. Oh- [LAUGHTER].

i_69_21.speaker: Speaker_M
i_69_21.utterance: I wonder if they can hear us talk. Or hear the vibrations in the sand when we move.

i_69_24.speaker: Speaker_F
i_69_24.utterance: It's past eleven, let's go home.

i_69_25.speaker: Speaker_M
i_69_25.utterance: What? Are you kidding me? We just got here. We'd miss it. What's the point of coming down there if we just turn around and go home?

i_69_26.speaker: Speaker_F
i_69_26.utterance: There is no point in coming down here.

i_69_31.speaker: Speaker_M
i_69_31.utterance: No. No, no, no, that's not my point. My point is there's no point in coming down here if we just turn around and leave.

i_69_32.speaker: Speaker_M
i_69_32.utterance: I mean, don't you want to see it?

i_69_35.speaker: Speaker_F
i_69_35.utterance: I don't know. I just don't.

i_69_36.speaker: Speaker_M
i_69_36.utterance: Honey, this is a natural-this is a natural phenomenon. I mean, it only happens once a year. This is, this is a great opportunity. Think about the people in Kansas. They would have to get a plane ticket and hotel reservation to come and see this. We're only forty minutes away. This is too good to pass up.

i_69_37.speaker: Speaker_F
i_69_37.utterance: Pass up what? A bunch of fish. They swim up to the beach and they flop around and they swim away and then they die.

i_69_38.speaker: Speaker_M
i_69_38.utterance: Yeah. But that moment on the beach is the highlight of their little lives. And all that flopping around is laying eggs or what you-spawning or mating or something like that.

i_69_39.speaker: Speaker_M
i_69_39.utterance: I mean, this is the big night for these guys. Life's last orgy.

i_69_40.speaker: Speaker_F
i_69_40.utterance: I don't know why you're getting so excited. I mean, it's not like the landing of the moon or something. It's just fish. Fish that nobody eats.

i_69_43.speaker: Speaker_M
i_69_43.utterance: What, okay, then it's fish that do something no other fish do. Like the swallows in Capistrano or the lemmings who throw themselves off the cliffs.

i_69_44.speaker: Speaker_M
i_69_44.utterance: There's magic here and mystery and a little bit of the unexplainable. I mean, I don't see how you cannot be interested.

i_69_45.speaker: Speaker_F
i_69_45.utterance: I'm just not. I mean, I'm sorry, it just-they're still fish to me.

i_69_48.speaker: Speaker_M
i_69_48.utterance: I don't get it. The last time-the first time that we came to see it you said it was the best night of your life.

i_69_49.speaker: Speaker_M
i_69_49.utterance: And then last year when we came to see it you ran out of the house so fast that you stubbed your toe. And you didn't even notice until you got in the car.

i_69_50.speaker: Speaker_F
i_69_50.utterance: Yes, and if you also remember, I got sand in my panties. We got into an argument about nothing. You got a sore throat and we never saw the grunions.

i_69_51.speaker: Speaker_M
i_69_51.utterance: We weren't in a good spot.

i_69_52.speaker: Speaker_F
i_69_52.utterance: No, for God's sakes, we've never seen the grunions. We're not going to see them.

i_69_53.speaker: Speaker_M
i_69_53.utterance: We just weren't in a good spot, all right? This is a much better spot. I have a good instinct for these things. Look, is that-

i_69_54.speaker: Speaker_M
i_69_54.utterance: no, that's just seaweed, isn't it? This is great, isn't it?

i_69_55.speaker: Speaker_M
i_69_55.utterance: I wouldn't miss this for anything. I mean, think about this. Okay, I know you're not interested, but just think about this for a minute.

i_69_56.speaker: Speaker_M
i_69_56.utterance: Somewhere out there there's this huge silver mass of fish heading this way. They don't know why. And we don't know why. Just some little instinctual time-release firecracker went off-went off in their bodies.

i_69_57.speaker: Speaker_M
i_69_57.utterance: And they all, all as one, turned as one and formed this line as big as the California coast and started swimming this way. Swimming as if that's the only thing that matters.

i_69_58.speaker: Speaker_M
i_69_58.utterance: You know, they didn't ask it a question. They didn't, they didn't take a vote. They didn't have an invitation or a map; they just started swimming as one.

i_69_59.speaker: Speaker_M
i_69_59.utterance: its single-mindedness with one mind. Urgency that's pure urge. You know, they don't give it a name.

i_69_60.speaker: Speaker_M
i_69_60.utterance: They don't ask it a question. They just, they just, they don't know what drives them. They just turn as one and start the swim that brings them to here

i_69_61.speaker: Speaker_M
i_69_61.utterance: where we stand for a massive celebration of sex and death. Oh, God, I'm giving myself goosebumps. Look, I'm all-all my hairs on my arms is sticking up.

i_70_2.speaker: Speaker_M
i_70_2.utterance: About what I was just saying.

i_70_4.speaker: Speaker_M
i_70_4.utterance: It certainly is not. It is slightly exaggerated scientific fact.

i_70_5.speaker: Speaker_F
i_70_5.utterance: It's so P.R. I mean, somebody's giving you a good bill about this wonderful event that's going to happen on the beach.

i_70_6.speaker: Speaker_M
i_70_6.utterance: Well, I'm not the only one. Look at all these people.

i_70_7.speaker: Speaker_F
i_70_7.utterance: Exactly. Everyone else is told the same thing. It keeps them feeling excited. Keeps them coming back. Keeps them thinking life's going to start at any minute now.

i_70_8.speaker: Speaker_M
i_70_8.utterance: Are we talking about the same thing?

i_70_9.speaker: Speaker_F
i_70_9.utterance: I don't care. It's still fish to me.

i_70_10.speaker: Speaker_M
i_70_10.utterance: Carla, can I ask you something? Don't take this wrong, but are you having your period?

i_70_11.speaker: Speaker_F
i_70_11.utterance: Augie, Goddamn it, don't ask me that. You always ask me that. Why do you ask me that? I hate it. It's insulting.

i_70_13.speaker: Speaker_F
i_70_13.utterance: Look, it's-we just-we're looking at this evening in a different way, both of us. We both have different ideas of it.

i_70_14.speaker: Speaker_M
i_70_14.utterance: There's nothing to it, alright? We come down here, the grunion arrive, they do their little fish business, we go ooh, ah, look at the fish, and then they go home and we go home.

i_70_17.speaker: Speaker_F
i_70_17.utterance: Look, Augie, we've never seen them. They're never going to come.

i_70_18.speaker: Speaker_M
i_70_18.utterance: We missed them twice. That's not ever.

i_70_19.speaker: Speaker_F
i_70_19.utterance: Twice is as many times as we've tried. That's ever.

i_70_22.speaker: Speaker_F
i_70_22.utterance: It's impossible. It's like waiting around. Waiting to see Santa Claus, you know?

i_70_23.speaker: Speaker_F
i_70_23.utterance: Like I feel like my whole life I've been just standing on this beach with my hands clasped waiting for these fish to show up and these fish never show up.

i_70_24.speaker: Speaker_M
i_70_24.utterance: God, I thought you wanted to see it.

i_70_26.speaker: Speaker_F
i_70_26.utterance: Look, I did. I always do. But it's like every time I try nothing ever happens.

i_70_27.speaker: Speaker_M
i_70_27.utterance: It's just fish. Nothing to get traumatized over.

i_70_28.speaker: Speaker_F
i_70_28.utterance: For God's sake, Augie, this is not about fish.

i_70_29.speaker: Speaker_M
i_70_29.utterance: I've been turning this thing over in my head trying to figure out when the point was when we took a turn for the worse, and I can't seem to pinpoint it.

i_70_30.speaker: Speaker_M
i_70_30.utterance: You were fine during the phone call from Marge. Dinner was okay. Doing the dishes was no problem. Let's see. I took a shower. You were watching the news. You said Augie, I think the grunion are running. I said great, let's go. I feel lucky tonight.

i_70_31.speaker: Speaker_M
i_70_31.utterance: And so I'm getting the animals, putting the animals out in the yard. I'm putting on my jacket, getting the car out of the garage-

i_70_32.speaker: Speaker_F
i_70_32.utterance: Do you remember the last time we came here?

i_70_34.speaker: Speaker_F
i_70_34.utterance: You brought a bottle of champagne.

i_70_35.speaker: Speaker_F
i_70_35.utterance: We took off our shoes and we danced barefoot in the sand. Under the moon.

i_70_36.speaker: Speaker_F
i_70_36.utterance: And you whispered so intimately in my ear so I could feel your words.

i_70_38.speaker: Speaker_F
i_70_38.utterance: I told myself finally, this is how happy I'm supposed to be.

i_70_39.speaker: Speaker_M
i_70_39.utterance: Wait a minute. I got it.

i_70_40.speaker: Speaker_F
i_70_40.utterance: Dancing barefoot in the sand. Drinking out of a bottle of champagne.

i_70_41.speaker: Speaker_M
i_70_41.utterance: It was driving past the Knickerbocker Liquor Locker, right? You said let's get some champagne, and I said time's-a-wasting or something like that.

i_70_42.speaker: Speaker_M
i_70_42.utterance: You want this to be like the other time, right? That's it. Right?

i_70_43.speaker: Speaker_F
i_70_43.utterance: I don't want to remember something.

i_70_45.speaker: Speaker_M
i_70_45.utterance: Right. I got it. Of course. Listen, do you want to dance?

i_70_47.speaker: Speaker_M
i_70_47.utterance: Do you want me to get some champagne?

i_70_51.speaker: Speaker_M
i_70_51.utterance: What? What? Do you want me to breathe in your ear?

i_70_53.speaker: Speaker_M
i_70_53.utterance: Do you want to get married again? What? What? Do you want to get a divorce?

i_70_59.speaker: Speaker_M
i_70_59.utterance: A-a back rub, some ice cream, a suicide pact. What the hell do you want, Carla?

i_70_60.speaker: Speaker_F
i_70_60.utterance: I want things to turn out the way that they're supposed to be.

i_70_61.speaker: Speaker_M
i_70_61.utterance: Jesus, welcome to the human race.

i_70_62.speaker: Speaker_M
i_70_62.utterance: Did you think this is how I thought it was going to turn out? Do you think I had some great fantasy down the line? That four years down the line that we'd come to the beach and argue with each other over fish?

i_70_63.speaker: Speaker_M
i_70_63.utterance: No, I was just like you. I thought it was going to be all peaches and roses and everything great. And the wind in our hair and the sand in our toes, and the fish will always come and the bills will pay themselves, and we'll have three big fat rosy kids. And oh God.

i_70_64.speaker: Speaker_M
i_70_64.utterance: You know what you get? The jokes on us, baby. Because do you know what you get? Huh? Carla? Do you know what you get? You get this.

i_70_65.speaker: Speaker_F
i_70_65.utterance: This what? What is this? This is nothing.

i_70_67.speaker: Speaker_F
i_70_67.utterance: No, it's not. It's standing on the beach waiting and fighting.

i_70_69.speaker: Speaker_F
i_70_69.utterance: Things have never turned out anything the way that I thought they would be.

i_70_71.speaker: Speaker_F
i_70_71.utterance: I mean, this is, this is everything, and it's a lot, but

i_70_72.speaker: Speaker_F
i_70_72.utterance: it's not something else. Do you know what I'm trying to say?

i_70_73.speaker: Speaker_M
i_70_73.utterance: Well, I'm doing the best I can.

i_70_74.speaker: Speaker_F
i_70_74.utterance: I know you are, Augie, and I know you wanted this evening to be nice, but I can't help thinking of being somewhere else.

i_70_75.speaker: Speaker_M
i_70_75.utterance: Yeah, maybe if you were with someone else too.

i_70_77.speaker: Speaker_M
i_70_77.utterance: No, I know. But I know I don't make you happy.

i_70_78.speaker: Speaker_F
i_70_78.utterance: Augie, anything I do I'd rather be doing with you.

i_70_80.speaker: Speaker_F
i_70_80.utterance: God, don't you know that? I mean, you're the one that probably wants to be with somebody else. Somebody that didn't take herself so seriously, could enjoy herself more.

i_70_81.speaker: Speaker_M
i_70_81.utterance: Actually now that you mention it, no, I don't.

i_70_82.speaker: Speaker_F
i_70_82.utterance: Well, I guess we're just in the wrong spart-spot with the right person.

i_70_83.speaker: Speaker_M
i_70_83.utterance: Seemed like a pretty good spot to me. I mean, look at the view of the moon we've got from here.

i_70_85.speaker: Speaker_F
i_70_85.utterance: You can see right over the water.

i_70_90.speaker: Speaker_F
i_70_90.utterance: At least we won't need glasses.

i_70_91.speaker: Speaker_M
i_70_91.utterance: Are you still cold? Do you want to go home?

i_70_92.speaker: Speaker_F
i_70_92.utterance: So I was thinking you might be right.

i_70_93.speaker: Speaker_F
i_70_93.utterance: Maybe this is the right place after all.

i_70_96.speaker: Speaker_M
i_70_96.utterance: If we're very quiet the fish might come.

i_71_5.speaker: Speaker_F
i_71_5.utterance: I mean, nobody knows we're here except for Freda and she wouldn't ring up.

i_71_8.speaker: Speaker_M
i_71_8.utterance: We're all right, aren't we darling? Whatever happens.

i_71_11.speaker: Speaker_F
i_71_11.utterance: It was bound to come sooner or later.

i_71_12.speaker: Speaker_M
i_71_12.utterance: Hello? Hello? What? Ugh, wrong number.

i_71_13.speaker: Speaker_F
i_71_13.utterance: [LAUGHTER] God, it sent shivers up my spine.

i_71_14.speaker: Speaker_M
i_71_14.utterance: things that should matter dreadfully don't seem to matter at all when one's happy, do they?

i_71_15.speaker: Speaker_F
i_71_15.utterance: Um terrible thing is one can't stay happy. Well, it's true. I mean, the whole business is a very poor joke.

i_71_17.speaker: Speaker_M
i_71_17.utterance: You mean that sacred and beautiful thing, love?

i_71_19.speaker: Speaker_M
i_71_19.utterance: What does it all mean? That's what I ask myself in my quest for ultimate truth. What does it all mean?

i_71_20.speaker: Speaker_F
i_71_20.utterance: Don't laugh at me. I'm serious.

i_71_21.speaker: Speaker_M
i_71_21.utterance: [LAUGHTER]. You mustn't be serious, my dear one, that's what they want.

i_71_23.speaker: Speaker_M
i_71_23.utterance: All the futile moralists who try to make life unbearable. Laugh at them. Be flippant. Laugh at all the sacred shibboleths. Flippancy brings out the acid in their damn sweetness and light.

i_71_24.speaker: Speaker_F
i_71_24.utterance: Well, if I laugh at everything I'm going to have to laugh at us too.

i_71_25.speaker: Speaker_M
i_71_25.utterance: Ooh, certainly you must. We're figures of fun, all right.

i_71_26.speaker: Speaker_F
i_71_26.utterance: So how long is it going to last? This ludicrous, overbearing love of ours?

i_71_28.speaker: Speaker_F
i_71_28.utterance: Shall we always want to bicker and fight?

i_71_29.speaker: Speaker_M
i_71_29.utterance: No. The fire will fade along with our passion.

i_71_30.speaker: Speaker_F
i_71_30.utterance: Oh dear, well, shall we want that?

i_71_31.speaker: Speaker_M
i_71_31.utterance: Depends on how well we play it.

i_71_32.speaker: Speaker_F
i_71_32.utterance: Well, what if happens if one of us is to die? Does the one that's left still laugh?

i_71_33.speaker: Speaker_M
i_71_33.utterance: Yes, yes. Yes, he must with all his might.

i_71_35.speaker: Speaker_M
i_71_35.utterance: No. No, it isn't. Death's very laughable. A cunning little mystery. All done with mirrors.

i_71_36.speaker: Speaker_F
i_71_36.utterance: Darling, I believe you're talking nonsense.

i_71_37.speaker: Speaker_M
i_71_37.utterance: So is everyone else in the long run. Let's be superficial and pity the poor philosophers.

i_71_38.speaker: Speaker_M
i_71_38.utterance: Let's blow strumpets, blow-blow trumpets and squeakers and party as much as we can like small quite idiotic school children. Let's savor the delight of the moment. Come, kiss me darling, before worms pop in and out of your eye sockets.

i_71_40.speaker: Speaker_M
i_71_40.utterance: I don't care what you do, see? As long-you can paint yourself bright green all over and dance naked in the Place Vendome and rush off madly with all the men in the world and I shan't say a word as long as you love me best.

i_71_41.speaker: Speaker_F
i_71_41.utterance: Thank you, dear. The same applies to you. Except that if I ever so much as catch you looking at another woman I'll kill you.

i_71_42.speaker: Speaker_M
i_71_42.utterance: Remember that awful scene we had in Venice?

i_71_44.speaker: Speaker_M
i_71_44.utterance: The one when you bought that little painted wooden snake in the Piazza and put it in my bed.

i_71_45.speaker: Speaker_F
i_71_45.utterance: Oh Charles. That was his name. Charles. He did wiggle so beautifully.

i_71_46.speaker: Speaker_M
i_71_46.utterance: Horrible little thing. I hated it.

i_71_47.speaker: Speaker_F
i_71_47.utterance: I know you did. You threw it out the window and into the Grand Canal. I don't think I'll ever forgive you for that.

i_71_48.speaker: Speaker_M
i_71_48.utterance: How long did that row last?

i_71_49.speaker: Speaker_F
i_71_49.utterance: It went on intermittently for days.

i_71_50.speaker: Speaker_M
i_71_50.utterance: The worst one was in Cannes when your-your curling iron burnt a hole in my dressing gown.

i_71_51.speaker: Speaker_F
i_71_51.utterance: Well, I burnt a hole in my comb too, and all the towels in the bathroom. [LAUGHTER].

i_71_52.speaker: Speaker_M
i_71_52.utterance: That was a rouser, wasn't it?

i_71_53.speaker: Speaker_F
i_71_53.utterance: yeah. And that was the first time you ever hit me.

i_71_54.speaker: Speaker_M
i_71_54.utterance: I didn't hit you very hard.

i_71_55.speaker: Speaker_F
i_71_55.utterance: The manager came in and found us rolling on the floor and biting and scratching like panthers. [LAUGHTER]

i_71_56.speaker: Speaker_M
i_71_56.utterance: Oh, I will never forget his face. Ooh-rah [LAUGHTER].

i_71_57.speaker: Speaker_F
i_71_57.utterance: I know. Oh man. Couldn't believe it.

i_71_58.speaker: Speaker_M
i_71_58.utterance: We were very much younger then.

i_71_60.speaker: Speaker_M
i_71_60.utterance: Oh, the real cause of that row was Peter Burden.

i_72_0.speaker: Speaker_M
i_72_0.utterance: As a matter of fact, the real cause of that row was Peter Burden.

i_72_1.speaker: Speaker_F
i_72_1.utterance: You knew there was nothing in that.

i_72_2.speaker: Speaker_M
i_72_2.utterance: I knew nothing of the sort. You took presents from him.

i_72_3.speaker: Speaker_F
i_72_3.utterance: Presents? It was only a small trivial little broach.

i_72_4.speaker: Speaker_M
i_72_4.utterance: I remember it well. Bristling with diamonds. In the worst possible taste.

i_72_5.speaker: Speaker_F
i_72_5.utterance: Not at all. It was very pretty. And I wear it all the time. I still have it.

i_72_6.speaker: Speaker_M
i_72_6.utterance: You went out of your way to torture me over Peter Burden.

i_72_7.speaker: Speaker_F
i_72_7.utterance: No, I didn't. You made that up all in your jealous imagination.

i_72_8.speaker: Speaker_M
i_72_8.utterance: You must admit that he was in love with you.

i_72_9.speaker: Speaker_F
i_72_9.utterance: Just a little perhaps, but it was nothing serious.

i_72_10.speaker: Speaker_M
i_72_10.utterance: You let him kiss you. You said so.

i_72_13.speaker: Speaker_F
i_72_13.utterance: Oh come on, it gave him a lot of pleasure and it didn't harm me.

i_72_15.speaker: Speaker_F
i_72_15.utterance: Well, if you hadn't been so suspicious and nosey you'd have never known anything about it.

i_72_16.speaker: Speaker_M
i_72_16.utterance: That's a nice point of view, I must say.

i_72_18.speaker: Speaker_M
i_72_18.utterance: Are you bored with this conversation?

i_72_20.speaker: Speaker_M
i_72_20.utterance: So am I. Bored stiff. Want some brandy?

i_72_22.speaker: Speaker_M
i_72_22.utterance: I'll have a little, I think.

i_72_23.speaker: Speaker_F
i_72_23.utterance: I don't see why you want it; you've already had two glasses.

i_72_24.speaker: Speaker_M
i_72_24.utterance: No particular reason. Anyhow, they were very small ones.

i_72_25.speaker: Speaker_F
i_72_25.utterance: I don't see what's the point of going on and on about something like this.

i_72_26.speaker: Speaker_M
i_72_26.utterance: Oh, my-you can hardly call three minute liquor glasses in the evening going on and on and on.

i_72_27.speaker: Speaker_F
i_72_27.utterance: It's become a habit with you.

i_72_28.speaker: Speaker_M
i_72_28.utterance: You needn't be so grand just because you don't happen to want some yourself at the moment.

i_72_34.speaker: Speaker_F
i_72_34.utterance: No. Just making myself fascinating for you.

i_72_35.speaker: Speaker_M
i_72_35.utterance: That reply has broken my heart.

i_72_36.speaker: Speaker_F
i_72_36.utterance: It's a woman's job to allure the man. Watch me, won't you, a minute?

i_72_37.speaker: Speaker_M
i_72_37.utterance: As a matter of fact, that's perfectly true.

i_72_40.speaker: Speaker_F
i_72_40.utterance: No, it's not. I don't care.

i_72_41.speaker: Speaker_M
i_72_41.utterance: It's a pity you didn't have more brandy. It might have made you a little less disagreeable.

i_72_42.speaker: Speaker_F
i_72_42.utterance: Well, it didn't seem to do such wonders with you, now did it?

i_72_43.speaker: Speaker_M
i_72_43.utterance: Oh, snap, snap, snap, like a little adder.

i_72_45.speaker: Speaker_M
i_72_45.utterance: Yes, they do. They have a big bag of venom behind their fangs and they snap.

i_72_48.speaker: Speaker_F
i_72_48.utterance: I don't care. I don't care, do you understand? I don't care if they bark, if they roll over on the floor like hoops.

i_72_50.speaker: Speaker_M
i_72_50.utterance: did you see much of Peter Burden after our divorce?

i_72_51.speaker: Speaker_F
i_72_51.utterance: Yes, I did, quite a lot actually.

i_72_52.speaker: Speaker_M
i_72_52.utterance: I imagine that you let him kiss you quite a bit more then, didn't you?

i_72_54.speaker: Speaker_M
i_72_54.utterance: You had a riotous-must have had a riotous time. No restraint at all. Very enjoyable. You never had much anyhow.

i_72_55.speaker: Speaker_F
i_72_55.utterance: You are quite insufferable. I suppose it's because you're drunk.

i_72_56.speaker: Speaker_M
i_72_56.utterance: I'm not in the least bit drunk.

i_72_57.speaker: Speaker_F
i_72_57.utterance: You've always had a weak head.

i_72_58.speaker: Speaker_M
i_72_58.utterance: Okay. As I mentioned before, I said I had three very minute liquor glasses. That's hardly enough to get a child of two drunk.

i_72_59.speaker: Speaker_F
i_72_59.utterance: On the contrary, a child of two can get violently drunk on only one glass of brandy.

i_72_60.speaker: Speaker_M
i_72_60.utterance: Oh, very interesting indeed. How about a child of four or a child of six or a child of nine. We might have a splendid little debate about this intemperate tots.

i_72_62.speaker: Speaker_F
i_72_62.utterance: I don't want to talk about this anymore.

i_72_65.speaker: Speaker_M
i_72_65.utterance: It's very late and it will annoy the people upstairs.

i_72_66.speaker: Speaker_F
i_72_66.utterance: There aren't any people upstairs. It's a photographer's studio.

i_72_67.speaker: Speaker_M
i_72_67.utterance: Well, I suppose the people downstairs then.

i_72_69.speaker: Speaker_M
i_72_69.utterance: This is no time of the year for Tunis.

i_72_71.speaker: Speaker_M
i_72_71.utterance: No, I will do no such thing.

i_72_74.speaker: Speaker_F
i_72_74.utterance: You've always had far too temperamental for yourself. You should try to control it.

i_72_76.speaker: Speaker_F
i_72_76.utterance: I will not. Go away. Go away. I hate you.

i_72_77.speaker: Speaker_M
i_72_77.utterance: Oh, very amusing, indeed. Amanda, listen-

i_72_78.speaker: Speaker_F
i_72_78.utterance: Listen, indeed. No, no, no, no. I am sick and tired of listening to you, you sadistic bully.

i_72_80.speaker: Speaker_F
i_72_80.utterance: Stop it, stop it, I hate you, I loath you, you are overbearing and conceited and utterly impossible.

i_72_81.speaker: Speaker_M
i_72_81.utterance: You know, you're a vile, ill-tempered, wicked little beast and I hope that I never see you again as long as I live.

i_72_82.speaker: Speaker_F
i_72_82.utterance: This is the end. Do you hear? The end. Now and forever.

i_72_83.speaker: Speaker_M
i_72_83.utterance: No, you're not going out like this. No you're not.

i_72_85.speaker: Speaker_F
i_72_85.utterance: Yes I am. Let go of me. I hate you and I loath you. God, I can't believe I just finally realized how you are. I am never marry you. Never. Never, never, never. I would rather die in torment.

i_72_86.speaker: Speaker_M
i_72_86.utterance: Oh, shut up. Shut up. Shut up.

i_72_87.speaker: Speaker_M
i_72_87.utterance: I wouldn't marry you again if you came back crawling to me on your hands and knees because you're vile. You're a mean, evil-minded little vampire and I hope to God I never set eyes on you again.

i_72_88.speaker: Speaker_F
i_72_88.utterance: Beast or brute cad, swine beast beast brute.

i_73_0.speaker: Speaker_M
i_73_0.utterance: Hey listen here missy, I've been in this line for an hour and now I'm finally here.  So you're going to take my application.

i_73_2.speaker: Speaker_M
i_73_2.utterance: It's not a demand it's a request.  Will you just take it?  Come on.

i_73_3.speaker: Speaker_F
i_73_3.utterance: I must see it before I just take it.

i_73_5.speaker: Speaker_F
i_73_5.utterance: Sir, you completely filled this out wrong.

i_73_7.speaker: Speaker_F
i_73_7.utterance: No.  You filled out the back part.  You were supposed to fill out the front portion only.

i_73_8.speaker: Speaker_M
i_73_8.utterance: What are you talking about?  It says fill out that part.

i_73_9.speaker: Speaker_F
i_73_9.utterance: No, no, no, no, no.  You're going to have to go back over on the other side-- --second door, up the stairs...

i_73_11.speaker: Speaker_M
i_73_11.utterance: Oh my God. Can you just transfer it yourself?

i_73_12.speaker: Speaker_F
i_73_12.utterance: No I can't just transfer it myself sir.  You got to do this.  Everyone else is doing the same thing.

i_73_13.speaker: Speaker_M
i_73_13.utterance: You don't know! you can't write?  You don't know how to write?

i_73_15.speaker: Speaker_M
i_73_15.utterance: What's wrong with you people?  God.

i_73_16.speaker: Speaker_F
i_73_16.utterance: I think you're being a little condescending.

i_73_17.speaker: Speaker_F
i_73_17.utterance: You need to go back over there and fill it out again.

i_73_18.speaker: Speaker_M
i_73_18.utterance: The information is on there.  Here I'll just fill it out right here.

i_73_19.speaker: Speaker_F
i_73_19.utterance: No, no, no, because there are other people in line.  You have to go get a complete new application.

i_73_20.speaker: Speaker_M
i_73_20.utterance: This will take like a minute, okay? Jesus.

i_73_21.speaker: Speaker_F
i_73_21.utterance: No, no, no. Sir,you need to get out of the line--

i_73_22.speaker: Speaker_M
i_73_22.utterance: I'm not going anywhere.  I'm staying right here.

i_73_23.speaker: Speaker_F
i_73_23.utterance: Well I'm going to have to call- security because sir, you- there are hundreds of people waiting in line at this D.M.V.-- --We're closing in about half an hour...

i_73_24.utterance: Hey,uh-- Well that's too bad cause I'm getting my license right now.

i_73_25.speaker: Speaker_F
i_73_25.utterance: You will get your license, sir, you have to go fill out-- --another copy...excuse me?

i_73_27.utterance: No.  Now I'm going to take this from you because you are obviously misleading

i_73_28.speaker: Speaker_M
i_73_28.utterance: Hey, no, give me that back.

i_73_29.speaker: Speaker_F
i_73_29.utterance: No.  You couldn't go get another one.

i_73_30.speaker: Speaker_M
i_73_30.utterance: -- No.  I'm not waiting...I'm not coming back again.  I don't have time to come back five days.

i_73_31.speaker: Speaker_F
i_73_31.utterance: Well then you won't have a license will you?

i_73_33.speaker: Speaker_F
i_73_33.utterance: Well not from this department you won't.

i_73_34.utterance: Yes I will.  I'll have this [GARBAGE]

i_73_39.utterance: Fine.  Call your supervisor.  Ask supervisor over

i_73_42.utterance: How would you like that hmm?

i_73_45.speaker: Speaker_M

i_73_46.utterance: Okay.  I'm going to call security. Security!

i_73_47.speaker: Speaker_F
i_73_47.utterance: I have it.  I'm taking it.  Doesn't mean I'm going to do anything with it.--Get out of my line!  This is my line.  Get out.  All these people--Sir, you are causing a mob in this department.

i_73_48.utterance: Okey This is a new one.  Take the new one.

i_73_51.utterance: No, You cannot get your license.  You filled out the wrong part of the form.

i_73_52.speaker: Speaker_M

i_114_21.utterance: Because she knows what I know, that's why.  She's faithful as a rock.  In my worst moments, I think of her waiting and I know, again, that I'm right.

i_114_22.speaker: Speaker_M
i_114_22.utterance: Look, it's a nice day.  What are we arguing for?

i_114_23.speaker: Speaker_F
i_114_23.utterance: Nobody in this house dares take her faith away, Joe.  Strangers might, but not his father, not his brother.

i_114_24.speaker: Speaker_M
i_114_24.utterance: What do you want me to do? What do you want?

i_114_25.speaker: Speaker_F
i_114_25.utterance: I want you to act like he's coming back, both of you.  Don't think I haven't noticed you since he invited her here.  I won't stand for any nonsense.

i_114_27.speaker: Speaker_F
i_114_27.utterance: Because if he's not coming back, I'll kill myself.

i_114_28.speaker: Speaker_F
i_114_28.utterance: Laugh.  Laugh at me.  But why did that happen the very night that she came here?

i_114_29.speaker: Speaker_F
i_114_29.utterance: She goes to sleep in his bed and his memorial breaks in pieces.  Look.  Look at it.  Joe--

i_114_31.speaker: Speaker_F
i_114_31.utterance: Believe with me, Joe.  I can't stand all alone

i_114_33.speaker: Speaker_F
i_114_33.utterance: Only last week, a man turned up in Detroit missing longer than Larry.

i_114_35.speaker: Speaker_F
i_114_35.utterance: You, above all, have got to believe.  You.

i_114_38.speaker: Speaker_M
i_114_38.utterance: What does that mean, me above all?  Look at you.  You're shaking.

i_114_40.speaker: Speaker_M
i_114_40.utterance: What have I got to hide?  What the hell is the matter with you, Kate?

i_115_0.speaker: Speaker_F
i_115_0.utterance: You're the only one I know who loves his parents.

i_115_1.speaker: Speaker_M
i_115_1.utterance: I know.  It went out of style, didn't it?

i_115_2.speaker: Speaker_F
i_115_2.utterance: It's okay.  It's a good thing.  You know, it's lovely here and the air is sweet.

i_115_3.speaker: Speaker_M
i_115_3.utterance: So you're not sorry you came?

i_115_4.speaker: Speaker_F
i_115_4.utterance: No, not sorry, but I'm not going to stay.

i_115_6.speaker: Speaker_F
i_115_6.utterance: In the first place, your mother as much as told me to go.

i_115_7.speaker: Speaker_F
i_115_7.utterance: You saw that and then you've been kind of--

i_115_9.speaker: Speaker_F
i_115_9.utterance: Well, kind of embarrassed ever since I got here.

i_115_10.speaker: Speaker_M
i_115_10.utterance: The trouble is I planned on sneaking up on you over a period of a week or so.  But they take it for granted that we're all set.

i_115_11.speaker: Speaker_F
i_115_11.utterance: I knew they would, your mother, anyway.

i_115_13.speaker: Speaker_F
i_115_13.utterance: From her point of view, why else would I come?

i_115_15.speaker: Speaker_M
i_115_15.utterance: I guess you know this is why I asked you to come.

i_115_16.speaker: Speaker_F
i_115_16.utterance: I guess this is why I came.

i_115_17.speaker: Speaker_M
i_115_17.utterance: Ann, I love you.  I love you a great deal.  I love you.

i_115_18.speaker: Speaker_M
i_115_18.utterance: I have no imagination.  That's all I know to tell you.  I'm embarrassing you.

i_115_19.speaker: Speaker_M
i_115_19.utterance: I didn't want to tell it to you here.  I wanted someplace we'd never been; someplace where we'd be brand new to each other.

i_115_20.speaker: Speaker_M
i_115_20.utterance: You think this is wrong, don't you; this yard, this chair?  I want you to be ready for me.  I don't want to win you away from anything.

i_115_21.speaker: Speaker_F
i_115_21.utterance: Chris, I've been ready a long, long time.

i_115_22.speaker: Speaker_M
i_115_22.utterance: Then he's gone forever?  You're sure?

i_115_23.speaker: Speaker_F
i_115_23.utterance: I almost got married two years ago.

i_115_26.speaker: Speaker_M
i_115_26.utterance: You felt something that far back?

i_115_28.speaker: Speaker_M
i_115_28.utterance: Anne, why didn't you let me know?

i_115_29.speaker: Speaker_F
i_115_29.utterance: I was waiting for you, Chris.  'Til then, you never wrote and when you did, what did you say?  You sure can be ambiguous, you know.

i_115_30.speaker: Speaker_M
i_115_30.utterance: Anne, give me a kiss.  Give me a -

i_115_31.speaker: Speaker_M
i_115_31.utterance: I kissed you.  I kissed Annie.  How long, how long I've waited to kiss you.

i_115_32.speaker: Speaker_F
i_115_32.utterance: I'll never forgive you.  Why did you wait all these years?  All I've done is sit around and wonder if I was crazy for thinking of you.

i_115_33.speaker: Speaker_M
i_115_33.utterance: Anne, we're going to live now.  I'm going to make you so happy.

i_115_36.speaker: Speaker_F
i_115_36.utterance: Like Larry's brother.  Do it like you, Chris.  What is it, Chris?

i_115_37.speaker: Speaker_M
i_115_37.utterance: Let's drive somewhere.  I want to be alone with you.

i_115_38.speaker: Speaker_F
i_115_38.utterance: No, what is it, Chris?  Your mother?

i_115_40.speaker: Speaker_F
i_115_40.utterance: Then what's wrong.  Even in your letters, there was something ashamed.

i_115_41.speaker: Speaker_M
i_115_41.utterance: Yes, I suppose there has been.  But it's going from me.

i_115_43.speaker: Speaker_M
i_115_43.utterance: I don't know where to start.

i_115_45.speaker: Speaker_M
i_115_45.utterance: It's all mixed up with so many other things.  You remember overseas, I was in command of a company?

i_115_51.speaker: Speaker_M
i_115_51.utterance: It takes a little time to toss that off because they weren't just men.

i_115_52.speaker: Speaker_M
i_115_52.utterance: For instance, one time it'd been raining several days.  And this kid came to me and gave me his last pair of dry socks; put them in my pocket.  That's only a little thing, but that's the kind of guys I had.

i_115_53.speaker: Speaker_M
i_115_53.utterance: They didn't die.  They killed themselves for each other.  I mean that exactly.  A little more selfish and they'd have been here today.

i_115_54.speaker: Speaker_M
i_115_54.utterance: And I got this idea watching them go down, everything was being destroyed, see?  But, to me, it seemed there was one new thing that was made,

i_115_55.speaker: Speaker_M
i_115_55.utterance: a kind of responsibility.  Man for man.  You understand me?  And then I went home and it was incredible.

i_115_56.speaker: Speaker_M
i_115_56.utterance: There was no meaning in it here.  The whole thing to them was some sort of bus accident.  Then I went to work for dad and that whole rat race again.  I felt, what you said, ashamed somehow

i_115_57.speaker: Speaker_M
i_115_57.utterance: because nobody was changed.  It seemed to make suckers out of a lot of guys.  I felt wrong to be alive;

i_115_58.speaker: Speaker_M
i_115_58.utterance: to open that bank book; to drive the new car; to see the new refrigerator.  I mean, you can take these things out of a war, but when you drive the new car, you have to know that it came out of the love a man can have for a man.

i_115_59.speaker: Speaker_M
i_115_59.utterance: You have to be a little better because of that.  Otherwise, we have is really loot and there's blood on it.  I don't want to take any of it.  I guess that included you.

i_115_60.speaker: Speaker_F
i_115_60.utterance: And do you still feel that way?

i_115_62.speaker: Speaker_F
i_115_62.utterance: Because you mustn't feel that way anymore, Chris.  Because you have a right to everything you have, everything, Chris; to me, too.

i_115_63.speaker: Speaker_F
i_115_63.utterance: And your money, there's nothing wrong in your money.  Your father put hundreds of planes in the air.  You should be proud.  A man should be paid for that.

i_115_64.speaker: Speaker_M
i_115_64.utterance: Annie, Annie, I'm going to make you a fortune.

i_115_65.speaker: Speaker_F
i_115_65.utterance: What'll I do with a fortune?

i_116_0.speaker: Speaker_M

i_116_1.utterance: Actually, I wanted to go a little farther up the coast, get away from the lights and the people, but I was afraid we'd miss it.  How you doing?

i_116_3.speaker: Speaker_M

i_116_4.speaker: Speaker_M

i_116_12.speaker: Speaker_M

i_116_13.speaker: Speaker_M

i_116_15.speaker: Speaker_M

i_116_16.speaker: Speaker_M

i_116_27.speaker: Speaker_M

i_116_31.speaker: Speaker_M

i_116_32.speaker: Speaker_F

i_116_33.speaker: Speaker_M

i_116_35.speaker: Speaker_M

i_116_44.speaker: Speaker_F

i_116_48.utterance: no, it's just seaweed, isn't it?  Oh, man, this is great.  I wouldn't miss this for anything.  I mean, think about this.  I know you're not interested, but just think about this for a minute.

i_116_49.utterance: Out there someplace is this huge mass of silver fish heading this way.  They don't know why.  We don't know why.  It wasn't a decision.  They didn't take a vote.  No one sent them an invitation or a map;

They just turn, as one, and start the swim that brings them here; to this place, where we stand, for a massive celebration of sex and death.  Oh, man, I'm giving myself goose bumps.  All my hairs are popping up.

i_116_54.utterance: Well so what did you think?

i_117_15.utterance: There's nothing to it.  We come down here.  The grunions arrive.  They do their little fish business.  We go ooh, ah, look at the fish.  They go home.  We go home.

i_117_16.utterance: For heaven's sake, we are not going to see the grunions.

i_117_23.speaker: Speaker_F
i_117_23.utterance: No, we won't.  It's pointless.  It's like waiting up to see Santa Claus.  I feel as though my entire life is going to be spent standing on the beach with my eyes wide open and my hands clasped expectantly waiting for the fish to show up.  And the fish won't show up.

i_117_24.speaker: Speaker_M
i_117_24.utterance: I thought you wanted to see them.

i_117_25.speaker: Speaker_F
i_117_25.utterance: You don't have any idea what I'm saying.

i_117_27.speaker: Speaker_F
i_117_27.utterance: I did want to see it.  I always do.  I keep thinking this time will be the time, but it never is.

i_117_28.speaker: Speaker_M
i_117_28.utterance: It's just fish; nothing to get traumatized over.

i_117_29.speaker: Speaker_F
i_117_29.utterance: For heaven's sake, this is not about fish.

i_117_30.utterance: I've been trying to work this backwards, but I can't seem to pinpoint the moment when we took a turn for the worse.

i_117_31.speaker: Speaker_M
i_117_31.utterance: You were fine during the phone call from Marge.  Dinner was okay.  Doing the dishes was no problem.  Let's see, I took a shower.  Uh, you watched the news.  You said the grunions are running tonight.  I said great, let's go.  I feel lucky tonight.

i_117_32.utterance: Uh,okay.  So I'm putting on my jacket; putting out the animals;

i_117_37.utterance: And while we were dancing, you said the softest, most intimate things to me, right into my ear so that I could feel them as much as hear them.  And I remember thinking this is it.  At last, at last I'm as happy as I'm supposed to be.

i_117_40.utterance: It was in the car, right?  Driving past the Knickerbocker Liquor Locker?  You said let's get some champagne and I said times a wasting or something like that.  Right?  That's it, right?  You want this to be like the other time.  That's it.  Of course.

i_117_44.utterance: You want to get some champagne?

i_117_46.speaker: Speaker_M
i_117_46.utterance: What, you want me to breathe in your ear?

i_117_48.speaker: Speaker_M
i_117_48.utterance: You want to get married again?

i_117_54.speaker: Speaker_M
i_117_54.utterance: A new carpet?  A poodle?  An ice cream?  A back rub?  A suicide pact?  What?  What, Carla?  What the hell do you want?

i_117_55.speaker: Speaker_F
i_117_55.utterance: I want something to turn out the way it's supposed to.

i_117_56.speaker: Speaker_M
i_117_56.utterance: Well welcome to the human race.  You think this is what I had in mind?  You think that when I proposed, I had this great fantasy going that four years down the line, we'd come to the beach and yell at each other over fish?  No.

i_117_57.speaker: Speaker_M
i_117_57.utterance: No, I'm just like you.  I thought it was going to be all peaches and roses and wind in our hair and sand in our toes and the fish will always come and the bills will always pay themselves.

i_117_58.utterance: And we'll have three fat rosy kids and ha, ha, ha, baby, the joke's on us, 'cause you know what you get, Carla?  You know what you get?  This.

i_117_59.utterance: This what?  What is this?  This isn't even anything.

i_117_61.utterance: Oh, right, sure.  This is standing on the beach.   This is waiting.  This is fighting.

i_117_63.utterance: But this isn't anything like I thought anything would be like.

i_117_68.utterance: Maybe if you were with somebody else, too, huh?
